steps.title: Identify the type of growth
steps.content: Harper starts with $110$ and earns $2.7\%$ more each year. One year later the amount is $110(1.027)=112.97$. Enter $x_1=[0,1]$, $y_1=[110,112.97]$, and $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$ in Desmos.

steps.title: Write the model
steps.content: Desmos gives $a=110$ and $b=1.027$, so $A(t)=110(1.027)^t$. Because $t$ is in the exponent, the model is exponential, not linear.

steps.title: Determine increasing or decreasing
steps.content:
The base $1.027 > 1$, so $A(t)$ grows as $t$ increases. The model is increasing exponential.
  • A (Decreasing exponential): The base exceeds 1, so the function increases, not decreases.
  • B (Decreasing linear): Growth is proportional (multiplicative), not additive, so it is not linear and it is not decreasing.
  • C (Increasing exponential): Multiplying by $1.027$ each year produces exponential growth. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D (Increasing linear): A fixed percentage applied to a changing balance produces exponential, not linear, growth.
